docs.title: Lagrangian melting front-tracking IBM documentation
docs.text:
This documentation provides a few technical details on the implementation of the melt_IBM code.

Most of the technical details on the method are prepared in the following article:

  • Zhong, K., Howland, C. J., Lohse, D. and Verzicco, R. A front-tracking immersed-boundary framework for simulating Lagrangian melting problems (under review for the Journal of Computational Physics). Available on arXiv here here

Some of the details in the article will be repeated here in this documentation for completeness. But the main purpose of this documentation is to highlight some more specific details not mentioned in the article (mostly specific to implementation) and to walkthrough some of the code.

This is a work in progress.

docs.title: LaTeX Math Support
docs.text:
LaTeX math is supported using MathJax.

Inline math looks like \(f(x) = x^2\). The input for this is $f(x) = x^2$. Use $...$.

For a block of math, use $$...$$ on separate lines

$$
F(x) = \int^a_b \frac{1}{2}x^4
$$

gives

\[ F(x) = \int^a_b \frac{1}{2}x^4 \]

docs.title: Mermaid diagrams
docs.text:
Here's the example from MkDocs Material documentation: 

graph LR
  A[Start] --> B{Error?};
  B -->|Yes| C[Hmm...];
  C --> D[Debug];
  D --> B;
  B ---->|No| E[Yay!];

docs.title: Text Formatting
docs.text:
You can have lists like this

  • first
  • second
  • third

Or checklist lists to

  • Get
  • things
  • done

Also, get highlights and strikethroughs as above (similar to Obsidian).

More formatting options for your webpage here. (but not compatible with Obsidian)
